## Summary

Fixes the mobile horizontal overflow shown on the RevisionGrade landing shell and hardens all shared landing pages against the same failure mode.

## What changed

- Collapses the desktop navigation behind a mobile `Menu` button below `lg` width.
- Adds a mobile nav panel with the same primary destinations.
- Adds a root/body horizontal overflow guard in the shared layout.
- Adds `app/mobile-responsive-guard.css` as a global safety net for shared landing surfaces.
- Adds a Playwright regression spec covering core public landing routes at a 390px mobile viewport.

## Routes covered by regression spec

- `/`
- `/pricing`
- `/revise`
- `/agent-readiness`
- `/storygate-studio`
- `/resources`
- `/black-box-problem`
- `/methodology`
- `/reliability`
- `/privacy-research-controls`
- `/security`
- `/genre-classification-faq`
- `/storygate-studio/faq`
- `/agent-readiness/faq`

## Acceptance criteria

- Mobile widths around 390px must not create page-level horizontal scrolling.
- Desktop nav must not be forced into the phone viewport.
- Hero copy remains visible without sideways swiping.
- Shared landing pages inherit the same overflow protection.
- Authenticated mobile users see a menu path to dashboard/evaluate/revise/sign out rather than an off-screen desktop nav.

## Notes

The root cause from the phone screenshots was the full desktop nav being rendered into a narrow viewport, creating a document wider than the device. This PR fixes that directly in `HeaderNav` and adds a shared page-level guard so other landing routes do not recreate the same issue.
